verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: If you wish me to keep silence, cease from accusing me.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Lay down your sword, and I will throw away my shield.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: To one thing only I cannot consent; that is, to spare the heretics, and not to vindicate my orthodoxy.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: If that is the cause of discord between us, I can submit to death, but not to silence.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: It would have been right to go through the whole of the Scriptures for answers to your ravings, and, like David playing on his harp, to take the divine words to calm your raging breast.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: But I will content myself with a few statements from a single book; I will oppose Wisdom to folly; for I hope if you despise the words of men you will not think lightly of the word of God.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Listen, then, to that which Solomon the wise says about you and all who are addicted to evil speaking and contumely:

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: “Foolish men, while they desire injuries, become impious and hate wisdom.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Devise not evil against thy friend.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Be not angry with a man without a cause.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: The impious exalt contumely.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Remove from thee the evil mouth, keep far from thee the wicked lips, the eyes of him that speaketh evil, the tongue of the unjust, the hands which shed the blood of the just, the heart that deviseth evil thoughts, and the feet which hasten to do evil.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: He that resteth upon falsehood feedeth the winds, and followeth the flying birds.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: For he hath left the ways of his own vineyard, and hath made the wheels of his tillage to err.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: He walketh through the dry and desert places, and with his hands he gathereth barrenness.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: The mouth of the froward is near to destruction, and he who uttereth evil words is the chief of fools.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Every simple man is a soul that is blessed; but a violent man is dishonourable.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: By the fault of his lips the sinner falleth into a snare.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: All the ways of a fool are right in his own eyes.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: The fool showeth his anger on that very day.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Lying lips are an abomination to the Lord.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: He that keepeth his lips guardeth his own soul; but he that is rash with his lips shall be a terror to himself.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: The evil man in his violence doeth evil things, and the fool spreadeth out his folly.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Seek for wisdom among the evil and thou shalt not find it.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: The rash man shall eat of the fruit of his own ways.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: The wise man by taking heed avoideth the evil; but the fool is confident, and joins himself to it.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: A long-suffering man is strong in his wisdom; the man of little mind is very unwise.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: He who oppresseth the poor reproacheth his Maker.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: The tongue of the wise knoweth good things, but the mouth of fools speaketh evil.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: A quarrelsome man preferreth strife, and every one that lifteth up his heart is unclean before God.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Though hand join with hand unjustly, they shall not be unpunished.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: He that loveth life must be sparing to his mouth.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Insolence goeth before bruising, and evil thoughts before a fall.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: He who closeth his eyes speaketh perverse things, and provoketh all evil with his lips.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: The lips of a fool lead him into evil, and the foolhardy speech calleth down death.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: The man of evil counsel shall suffer much loss.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Better is a poor man who is just than a rich man that speaketh lies.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: It is a glory to a man to turn away from evil words; but he that is foolish bindeth himself therewith.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Love not detraction, lest thou be rooted out.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: The bread of lying is sweet to a man, but afterwards his mouth shall be filled with gravel.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: He that gaineth treasures with a lying tongue followeth vanity, and shall come into the snares of death.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Say thou nought in the ear of a fool, lest haply the wise mock at thy words.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: The bludgeon and the sword and the arrow are hurtful things; so is the man who beareth false witness against his friend.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: As the birds and the sparrows fly away, so the curse shall be vain and shall not overtake him.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Answer not an unwise man according to his lack of wisdom, lest thou become like unto him; but answer a fool according to his folly, lest he appear to himself to be wise.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: He who layeth wait for his friends when he is discovered saith, I did it in sport.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: A faggot for the coals, and wood for the fire, and a man of evil words for the tumult of strife.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: If thine enemy ask thee aught, sparingly but with a loud voice, consent thou not to him, for there are seven degrees of wickedness in his heart.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: The stone is heavy, and the sand hard to be borne; but the anger of a fool is heavier than either; indignation is cruel, anger is sharp, and envy is impatient.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: The impious man speaketh against the poor; and he that trusteth in the audacity of his heart is most foolish.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: The unwise man putteth forth all his anger, but the wise dealeth it out in parts.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: An evil son—his teeth are swords, and his grinders are as harrows, to consume the weak from off the earth, and the poor from among men.”

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Such are the lessons in which I have been trained and therefore I was unwilling to return bite for bite, and to attack you by way of retaliation; and I thought it better to exorcise the madness of one who was raving, and to pour in the antidote of a single book into his poisoned breast.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: But I fear I shall have no success, and that I shall be compelled to sing the song of David, and to take his words for my only consolation:

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: “The wicked are estranged from the womb, they go astray even from the belly.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: They have spoken lies.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Their madness is like the madness of the serpent; like the deaf adder which stoppeth her ears, which will not hear the voice of the charmers, and of the magician wisely enchanting.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: God shall break their teeth in their mouth; the Lord shall break the great teeth of the lions.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: They shall come to nothing, like water that runneth away.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: He bendeth his bow until they be brought low.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Like wax that melteth, they shall be carried away; the fire hath fallen upon them and they have not seen the sun.”

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: “The righteous shall rejoice when he seeth the vengeance upon the impious; he shall wash his hands in the blood of the sinner.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: And man shall say, Verily, there is a reward for the righteous; verily, there is a God that judgeth those that are on the earth.”
